detailed_explanation: Mutual, reciprocal, partner. Prefix for shared actions. Used in compound words.
example_sentences: 相談する - Consult together
相手 - Partner/opponent
相思相愛 - Mutual love
相違 - Difference/discrepancy
usage_notes: 相〜 prefix indicates mutual/reciprocal action. Common in business/social contexts.

detailed_explanation: As always, unchanged from before. Often slightly negative/nostalgic connotation.
example_sentences: 相変わらず元気だね - You're energetic as always
相変わらず遅刻した - Late as usual
相変わらずの景色 - Same old scenery
usage_notes: 相変わらず = (time passes but) unchanged. Often implies criticism or nostalgia.

detailed_explanation: Greeting, bow, social courtesy. Essential in Japanese social harmony.
example_sentences: 挨拶する - Give greeting
朝の挨拶 - Morning greeting
挨拶回り - Round of visits/greetings
usage_notes: 挨拶 = crucial Japanese custom. New neighbors, business, New Year all require 挨拶.

detailed_explanation: Space/time between, interval, gap. While X is happening.
example_sentences: 仕事の間 - During work
東京と大阪の間 - Between Tokyo and Osaka
間に合う - Be in time
usage_notes: 間 = between. 間に = in time to. この間 = the other day/recently.

detailed_explanation: To happen in succession, occur repeatedly, come one after another.
example_sentences: 事故が相次ぐ - Accidents occur one after another
相次ぐ請求 - Successive claims
相次いで辞任 - Resign in succession
usage_notes: 相次ぐ = succession of similar events. News/accidents/resignations often 相次ぐ.

detailed_explanation: Unfortunately; expressing regret. Polite refusal starter.
example_sentences: 生憎、その日は都合が悪いです - Unfortunately I'm not available
生憎ながら在庫がありません - Regrettably out of stock
あいにく雨が降った - Unfortunately it rained
usage_notes: 生憎ながら = formal. N2 review from N3. Used for declining invitations/requests.

detailed_explanation: To be mutually dependent, wait for one another. Happen together/interdependently.
example_sentences: 相俟って完成する - Completed by working together
成功は努力と運が相俟つ - Success requires both effort and luck
usage_notes: 相俟つ = literary/formal. Two factors working together. Common in formal writing.

detailed_explanation: Using habitually for long time, favorite (product/brand).
example_sentences: 愛用の万年筆 - Favorite fountain pen
10年愛用している - Used for 10 years
愛用者 - Regular user/fan
usage_notes: 愛用 = habitual long-term use with affection. Customer testimonials use 愛用.

detailed_explanation: To mix ingredients (especially salad), dress with sauce.
example_sentences: サラダを和える - Dress salad
和え物 - Dressed dishes
ごま和え - Sesame-dressed
usage_notes: 和える = specifically food mixing. Contrast: 混ぜる (general mixing). Cooking term.

detailed_explanation: To look up at; respect/admire (person); drink (liquor - rare/archaic).
example_sentences: 空を仰ぐ - Look up at sky
先生を仰ぐ - Respect teacher
後ろ髮を引かれる - Reluctant to leave (lit: hair pulled from behind)
usage_notes: 仰ぐ = look up. 仰ぐ respect = hold in high esteem. Formal/polite contexts.

detailed_explanation: Boredom, being tired of, losing interest from repetition.
example_sentences: 飽きるほど見た - Saw until bored
飽き性 - Get bored easily
飽きもせず - Without getting bored
usage_notes: 飽きる = get bored. 飽きっぽい = gets bored easily (personality). 飽きるほど = ad nauseam.

detailed_explanation: Clearly obvious, apparent, not hidden. Clear to anyone.
example_sentences: 明らかに間違っている - Clearly wrong
明らかな差 - Obvious difference
明らかにする - Make clear/public
usage_notes: 明らか (na-adj). 明らかに (adv). N2 review from N3. = clear/obvious/known.

detailed_explanation: Giving up, resignation, abandoning hope. Noun form of 諦める.
example_sentences: 諦めが悪い - Can't give up easily
諦めつく - Come to accept/give up
諦めは早い - Give up quickly
usage_notes: 諦め = noun (resignation). 諦めがつく = come to terms with. 諦めが悪い = persistent.

detailed_explanation: To get tired of, lose interest in, bored from repetition.
example_sentences: すぐ飽きる - Get bored quickly
飽きずに続ける - Continue without getting bored
飽きた顔 - Bored expression
usage_notes: 飽きる = get bored. 飽きずに = without getting bored. Contrast: 疲れる (physical tired).

detailed_explanation: To open, become open. Intransitive. Happens naturally.
example_sentences: ドアが開く - Door opens
店が開く - Store opens
開いてる - Is open
usage_notes: 開く (intran) N2 review. Contrast: 開ける (tran). 開く for business opening.

detailed_explanation: Fussing anxiously, worrying over small things. Working busily but inefficiently.
example_sentences: あくせくしないで - Don't fuss
あくせく働く - Work anxiously/busily
あくせくする性格 - Fussy personality
usage_notes: あくせく = fuss/worry unnecessarily. Negative connotation of being overly anxious.

detailed_explanation: Yawn. Sign of tiredness or boredom.
example_sentences: 欠伸をする - Yawn
欠伸が出る - Yawn comes out
欠伸を噛む - Stifle yawn
usage_notes: 欠伸 = yawn. 噛む (suppress) yawn = polite. Common social behavior in Japan.

detailed_explanation: Finally, in the end (often with negative result). After much effort/time.
example_sentences: 挙句に逃げた - In the end ran away
考えた挙句 - After thinking (decided)
挙句の果てに - At the very end
usage_notes: 挙句(に) = after all that. Often negative. 挙句の果て = final result. Formal/written.

detailed_explanation: Day after tomorrow. Two days from now.
example_sentences: 明後日来る - Come day after tomorrow
明後日までに - By day after tomorrow
明後日の天気 - Day after tomorrow's weather
usage_notes: 明後日 = day after tomorrow. 明後日に = N2 re-review. Time expressions.

detailed_explanation: Leg, foot; means of transport; pace/gait; clue (police).
example_sentences: 足が痛い - Leg hurts
足が早い - Fast walker
足を洗う - Quit crime (wash feet)
足取り - Gait/person's movements
usage_notes: 足 = leg/foot. 足が早い = fast pace. 足を洗う = quit yakuza. 足取り = tracking clues.

detailed_explanation: Footprints, tracks; evidence of presence. One's path/career.
example_sentences: 雪に足跡 - Footprints in snow
足跡をたどる - Follow footprints
足跡を残す - Leave behind legacy
usage_notes: 足跡 = footprints literal or metaphorical. 足跡を残す = leave mark on history.
